hawkish_phrases: domestic inflation was still high, driven mainly by high services inflation
some members noted that a case could be made for a 50 basis point rate cut...These members emphasised the deterioration...A larger rate cut would provide insurance
it was too early to cut rates by a larger amount
risks to inflation appeared to have become more two-sided
the Governing Council should not let its guard down in the final stretch of disinflation
dovish_phrases: members were increasingly confident that inflation would return to target in the first half of 2025, which was earlier than had been foreseen
the risks to economic growth remained tilted to the downside
if the economy did not pick up, the likelihood would increase that inflation would undershoot the target further down the road
staff now expected a slower economic recovery than in the September projections, marking another downward adjustment in the growth outlook
the dialling-back of policy restriction over the past few months was also being transmitted to financing conditions
summary: The ECB cut rates by 25 basis points unanimously in December 2024, removing the tightening bias from its forward guidance with a shift to ensuring inflation 'stabilises sustainably at 2%', amid increasing confidence inflation will return to target in H1 2025 but with growth risks tilted to the downside and some members favouring a 50bp cut.
